summary: Adds Supabase-backed hub coordination storage (SPEC-V1-INITIATIVE Phase 4.3). A new migration creates the hub_* tables with append-heavy children (channel messages, proposal reviews, consensus endorsements) normalized into rows, a version column for optimistic concurrency on the remaining aggregates, tenant_workspace_id scoping, and workspace-membership RLS policies; it also drops the never-written hub_events/hub_tasks/hub_workers tables. SupabaseHubStorage implements the HubStorage interface (extended with appendMessage/appendReview/appendEndorsement) and replaces the interim per-tenant filesystem scoping in multi-tenant mode; local mode keeps FileSystemHubStorage. database.types.ts is regenerated from the local stack, and src/protocol/handler.ts now passes the workspace_id the regenerated types require.

claims.statement: Hub state is durable in Supabase in hosted mode, and concurrent writers on different instances do not lose appends (messages, reviews, endorsements).
claims.evidence_description: Two SupabaseHubStorage instances append 10 messages and 4 reviews concurrently with no lost writes; concurrent endorsements are all retained; a stale aggregate save throws instead of silently overwriting; a fresh instance reads the full state back from Postgres.

claims.statement: Tenant isolation: a SupabaseHubStorage scoped to one tenant workspace cannot see another tenant's hub workspaces, problems, or channels.
claims.evidence_description: Two instances with different tenant_workspace_ids each see only their own hub workspaces; cross-tenant gets return null/empty.

claims.statement: FileSystemHubStorage still satisfies the full HubStorage contract, including the new append operations, for local single-writer mode.
claims.evidence_description: The shared HubStorage contract suite runs against both backends; hub (218) and code-mode (41) suites pass.
